document_title: Cook Inlet gas storage, the RCA's no, and AI's gigawatt

01.dek: And the valve that just shut.

02.headline: Five parts move gas from rock to furnace
02.body: Reservoir trap, wellhead, pipeline, storage cavern, and the state regulator's valve. Part four is a battery for deep winter. Right now, it sits empty.

03.headline: Enough for an average day. Not for January.
03.body: Cook Inlet's aging fields can meet a normal day, but not a deep-cold peak, and there is little storage to bridge the gap. A former state senator's op-ed warns of a Southcentral shortfall by 2027.

04.headline: Turn a dead reservoir into a winter battery
04.body: Enstar proposed buying the depleted Kenai Loop Pool from AIX Energy and refilling it to hold 25 billion cubic feet, close to half a year's gas, for about $240 million and up to $12 a month on the average bill.

05.headline: Every fix runs through one valve
05.body: The Regulatory Commission of Alaska decides whether a utility's plan is prudent before customers ever pay for it.

06.headline: The regulator shut the valve
06.body: On July 10 the RCA denied the plan, calling it an "extraordinary request" that "lacked basic information about the project." The order ran 17 pages. Enstar's president said the company suspended the project.

07.headline: Can it hold?
07.intro: Three questions the fix could not answer. Any power promise should have to.

rows.a: Even half a year of storage was denied. (c6, c1)
The state has not confirmed the pool can store gas, and two firms claim the same lease. (c7, c8)
About $12 a month, and the project is now suspended. (c4, c9)

08.headline: Now measure what wants to plug in
08.body: One proposed North Slope AI campus, from STAK Energy, would generate "a gigawatt or more, some 30% more than urban Alaska's peak demand." More than $10 billion at full buildout. Its comment window closes July 17.
08.guardrail: Gas storage and power demand are different units, not one sum.
08.context: STAK lease: 500+ comments, fewer than a dozen in favor (c15)

09.headline: Store one winter first?
09.ask: Should Alaska approve a gigawatt of AI load before it can store a single winter of gas?
